summary: Iteration 58 P0 features tested: Items route split, Bulk Photo Uploader, WhatsApp webhook + delivery status endpoints, Settings whatsapp_verify_token/app_secret. Backend: 23/23 pytest tests pass. Frontend: all data-testids present and working on Items, Contracts, Settings pages. Login OK, bulk-photo dialog opens on every kind tab (car/motorcycle/electronic/pezadu), Contracts renders 412 WhatsAppStatusPills, Settings persists new fields via PUT /api/settings.

integration_issues.issue: One console error 'Failed to load resource: 400' observed during Contracts page load — non-blocking (pills still render for 412 rows). Likely a spurious 400 from an ancillary call; the /api/whatsapp/status bulk endpoint itself returns 200 in backend tests. Worth an E1 log check but not user-visible.

action_items: Investigate the transient 400 console error on /contracts page load; probable candidate is one of the auxiliary fetches (not /whatsapp/status which passed backend tests).

Optional (non-blocking): the Items table has hundreds of TEST_ items polluting the seed dataset from previous iterations (car tab shows 1941 items missing photos). Consider a one-off cleanup script for demo hygiene.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/items.py — clean, focused, ~212 lines. Kind is validated in a helper; models correct. No _id leaked.

routes/whatsapp.py — _apply_status_update correctly enforces monotonic sent→delivered→read while allowing 'failed' to always apply; stamp fields (sent_at, delivered_at, read_at, failed_at) preserved even when status doesn't move forward. Good.
routes/whatsapp.py — GET /whatsapp/webhook returns 503 when no verify token configured (matches request). Signature validation uses hmac.compare_digest — timing-safe. Well done.
server.py is still ~2017 lines even after the items split — consider continuing the split for contracts/settings/reminders as follow-up.

The whatsapp_app_secret + whatsapp_verify_token are returned in plaintext from GET /settings (whereas whatsapp_token is masked). Not a functional bug for now but consider masking or gating to admin-only for parity with existing token handling.
PATCH /items/{kind}/{iid}/photo dependency is require_not_cashier — matches request. write_audit is invoked. Good.

In routes/whatsapp.py _apply_status_update: minor — when the status is unchanged (order_new <= order_cur) and stamp already exists, the loop still increments through every row without break; that's fine because different log rows may need per-status stamps, but worth a comment.
Frontend Contracts.js polls /whatsapp/status every 30 s — no explicit cleanup shown in the imports scanned; ensure setInterval is cleared on unmount (not verified here).
seed_data_creation: Test creates transient items via POST /api/items/{kind} and deletes them in fixtures. No permanent seed data added.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iter58_items_whatsapp.py — covers Items CRUD refactor, PATCH photo endpoint, WhatsApp webhook GET/POST (with and without HMAC), status endpoints, and Settings token roundtrip. All 23 pass. Frontend confirmed via Playwright: item-bulk-photo-{car|motorcycle|electronic|pezadu}, bulk-photo-dialog, bulk-photo-dropzone, bulk-photo-cancel, bulk-photo-upload-all, bulk-photo-row-* all present; wa-pill-* renders on Contracts; settings-wa-verify-token, settings-wa-app-secret, wa-webhook-url all functional on Settings. Cleanup: after UI tests the verify_token/app_secret settings were reset to empty via PUT /api/settings. Backend regression tests baseline still around 418 passed / 20 failed — new suite doesn't add failures.
